sections.title: Housing Partners & Case Managers
sections.metaTitle: Housing Partners & Case Managers | Cleveland Section 8 Rentals
sections.metaDescription: Case managers, housing navigators, shelters, and social-service agencies: place voucher clients faster in Cleveland homes that welcome Housing Choice Vouchers. Partner with our local team.
sections.heroSub: If you help people secure or use Housing Choice Vouchers, we help you place them. Every home we work with welcomes Section 8 and is HUD-inspection-ready.
sections.answerBox: Rent Finder Cleveland is a local rental team whose entire Cleveland-area selection welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready. Case managers, housing navigators, shelters, resettlement agencies, and other social-service partners can send us a client's needs and we'll match them to available voucher-friendly homes and coordinate showings — no source-of-income screening, no runaround.
intro: Placing a client with a voucher shouldn't mean cold-calling dozens of landlords who quietly screen out vouchers. Every home in our current selection accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is ready for a CMHA HQS inspection, so your clients start from 'yes.'

We work with case managers, housing navigators, shelter and transitional-housing staff, refugee and resettlement agencies, reentry programs, domestic-violence advocates, and Area Agency on Aging coordinators across Greater Cleveland. Tell us what a client needs — bedrooms, neighborhood, timing, accessibility — and we'll point you to what's available and set up a tour.
sections.formSub: Send your details and we'll set up a partner contact. Fair-housing compliant; we never screen by source of income.

faq.q: Do you charge case managers or agencies to work with you?
faq.a: No. There's no cost to partner with us or to refer a client. Standard tenant application steps still apply to the renter once they choose a home.

faq.q: How fast can you place a voucher client?
faq.a: It depends on current availability and the client's needs, but because every home we work with already accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, you skip the hardest step — finding a landlord who says yes. Send us the details and we'll tell you what's available now.

faq.q: Do you screen out Housing Choice Voucher holders?
faq.a: Never. We welcome voucher holders on every available home, and we follow the Fair Housing Act. Where local source-of-income protections apply (e.g. Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, Linndale), we comply fully; we welcome vouchers everywhere we operate regardless.

sections.title: Corporate & Relocation Leasing
sections.metaTitle: Corporate & Relocation Leasing in Cleveland, OH | Rent Finder Cleveland
sections.metaDescription: Housing employees or relocating staff in Greater Cleveland? Tell us your team's housing needs and our local rental team will see how we can help. Corporate leasing inquiries welcome.
sections.heroSub: Housing a team in Greater Cleveland? Tell us what your employees or relocating staff need and we'll see how we can help with local rental homes.
sections.answerBox: Rent Finder Cleveland is a local Cleveland-area rental team. If your company needs to house relocating employees, project staff, or new hires in Greater Cleveland, tell us the details — number of people, timing, neighborhoods, and budget — and we'll let you know how our available rental homes can help. Every home we work with welcomes Housing Choice Vouchers as well.
intro: Relocating people to Cleveland — new hires, project crews, traveling staff, or transferring employees — is easier with a local contact who knows the market and the neighborhoods. Instead of piecing together listings, send us your team's requirements and we'll tell you what's realistic from our current selection of Greater Cleveland rental homes.

We're a local rental team, not a national relocation vendor, so we'll be straight with you about what we can and can't do. If we're a fit, we'll help with available homes and coordinate showings; if your needs are outside what we work with, we'll say so.
sections.formSub: Share a few details and our team will follow up to see how we can help.

faq.q: Do you provide furnished or short-term corporate housing?
faq.a: We're a local rental team, and availability varies. Rather than over-promise, tell us exactly what you need — furnished vs. unfurnished, length of stay, number of people, and neighborhoods — and we'll tell you honestly what our current selection can and can't cover.

faq.q: Can you house multiple employees at once?
faq.a: Sometimes, depending on current availability. Send us the headcount, timing, and preferred areas and we'll let you know what's realistic from the homes we work with right now.

faq.q: What areas of Greater Cleveland do you cover?
faq.a: Our selection is concentrated on Cleveland's East and Southeast sides, with additional homes in nearby suburbs and cities like Akron, Lorain, and Elyria. Tell us where your team needs to be and we'll tell you what's available nearby.

sections.title: Section 8 Landlord Services in Cleveland, OH
sections.metaTitle: Section 8 Landlord Services in Cleveland, OH
sections.metaDescription: Own a rental in Greater Cleveland? We handle the Section 8 paperwork end to end — listing, showings, RFTA, inspection and HAP contract. You sign twice. No placement, no fee.
sections.heroSub: Section 8 pays on time, every month. The paperwork is why most owners never start — so we carry it, and you sign twice.
sections.answerBox: Rent Finder Cleveland handles the Housing Choice Voucher process for property owners in Greater Cleveland: we list the unit, screen inquiries, run showings in person, file the RFTA with CMHA, schedule and attend the inspection, and carry it through to a signed HAP contract. Placement costs one month's rent, charged only after a tenant is placed. Inspection attendance is $100. Ongoing management is 5% of monthly rent while the unit is rented.
sections.serviceName: Section 8 leasing and property management for owners

offers.name: Tenant placement
offers.description: We list the unit, screen inquiries, run showings in person, and carry the voucher paperwork through to a signed HAP contract.
offers.priceNote: One month's rent, charged only once a tenant is placed. If no placement happens, there is no fee.

offers.name: Inspection attendance and report
offers.description: We meet the inspector at the property and send you the written result. You can also attend yourself instead.

offers.name: Ongoing property management
offers.description: Tenant communication, rent collection or pass-through, work orders, and repair estimates back inside 24 hours.
offers.priceNote: 5% of monthly rent, charged only while the unit is rented.

intro: Housing Choice Voucher rent is paid by the housing authority on a set schedule, which is why owners who use it tend to keep using it. The reason most owners never start is the process in between: the Request for Tenancy Approval, the inspection, the HAP contract, and the waiting in between each one.

We do that part. You sign a leasing permit so we can act for you, and later you sign the lease and the HAP contract. Everything between those two signatures is ours — and if the placement never happens, there is no fee to pay.
sections.formSub: Send your details and our leasing team will call you back. We work across Greater Cleveland (Cuyahoga County, under CMHA).

faq.q: What does it cost to have you place a Section 8 tenant?
faq.a: One month's rent, and it is charged only after a tenant is actually placed and the HAP contract is signed. If the placement never happens, you pay nothing. The only thing you sign up front is a leasing permit, which is what lets us act for you with CMHA.

faq.q: I already found a tenant. Can you just handle the paperwork?
faq.a: Yes. We can file the Request for Tenancy Approval, follow it through the housing authority, and schedule the inspection. Attending the inspection and sending you the written report is $100. If you would rather attend it yourself, you can — you only pay if we go.

faq.q: What does ongoing management cover, and what does it cost?
faq.a: 5% of monthly rent, charged only while the unit is rented. That covers tenant communication, collecting rent or routing it straight to you, taking work orders, and getting repair estimates back to you inside 24 hours.

faq.q: What if the unit fails inspection?
faq.a: A failed inspection is a delay, not a dead end. Send us the report and we return a free, itemized estimate inside 24 hours. Nothing gets repaired without your approval first.

Greater Cleveland — Cuyahoga County, where CMHA is the housing authority. That includes Cleveland, East Cleveland, Euclid, Parma, Lakewood, Cleveland Heights, Shaker Heights and the surrounding suburbs.

faq.q: Do I have to accept Housing Choice Vouchers?
faq.a: Whether an owner may decline a voucher depends on local law, and the rules differ between Ohio as a whole and individual cities. Check the current ordinance for the municipality your property sits in, or ask us and we will point you to it. What we can say is that owners who do participate get rent paid on a set schedule by the housing authority.
